channel.title: Editing single.php from WP install or frontity?
channel.description: I need to add a code snippet to the WP single.php file. From my experience with WP this would always obviously be done in the WP install single.php, however with Frontity, is there a way to add a php snippet to the blog.js file or would I need to add it to the single.php file in the WP install? This might not make sense, so I can expound if needed.

item.title: Editing single.php from WP install or frontity?
item.dc:creator: juanma
item.description:
Hi @meaghan1,

If what you want to do is displaying popular posts with Frontity you first need to add that info to the REST API. Here you have a related answer for getting this:

Getting popular posts 🤗 Dev Talk & Questions
Hi @meaghan1, As long as this information is in the REST API you should be able to display them properly with Frontity You can use the views metadata to detect popular posts. Here you have an article that may be useful to add this info to the REST API Hope this helps.

Hope this helps.

Read full topic
item.source: Editing single.php from WP install or frontity?

item.title: Editing single.php from WP install or frontity?
item.dc:creator: meaghan1
item.description:
The reason for needing to add code to the single.php file is to add the ability to view post views counter. I am using this article: https://blog.hubspot.com/website/wordpress-post-views. But if anyone has a better way to accomplish this that would also be helpful.

Read full topic
item.source: Editing single.php from WP install or frontity?

item.title: Editing single.php from WP install or frontity?

item.description:
I need to add a code snippet to the WP single.php file. From my experience with WP this would always obviously be done in the WP install single.php, however with Frontity, is there a way to add a php snippet to the blog.js file or would I need to add it to the single.php file in the WP install? This might not make sense, so I can expound if needed.
